name: Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd
oneLine: Test/QA environments named in CT (adfstest, qa.convenience, qa.speed)

emailAuth.note: SPF ~all soft-fail; Cloudflare 403 unbypassable for header verification

findings: ADFS test endpoint exposed in CT logs (adfstest.bharatpetroleum.in)
QA convenience and speed-store environments named publicly
Cloudflare 403 prevents external header verification — Phase 2 active scan needed

fingerprints.stack: Cloudflare WAF + DigiCert TLS + Microsoft stack (ADFS, Teams, Exchange)
eolFlags: External header analysis blocked by Cloudflare 403
Test environments expose architecture; credentials often reused from prod

attackPaths.name: ADFS brute force + credential spray
attackPaths.entry: adfs.bharatpetroleum.in form-based login enables user enumeration via error messages.
attackPaths.pivot: Username list + spray (breached lists, SIM swap). MFA bypass if SMS-based.

attackPaths.name: QA environment exploitation → production pivot
attackPaths.entry: adfstest, qa.convenience, qa.speed have weaker posture (default creds, disabled auth, permissive CORS common in QA).
attackPaths.pivot: QA ADFS access → test user DB → spray production ADFS. QA portals may carry embedded API keys.
attackPaths.objective: Credential harvest; lateral move to prod with months of dwell time.

attackPaths.name: Citrix NetScaler pre-auth RCE (if rpad/connectbpc on NetScaler)
attackPaths.entry: rpad / connectbpc endpoints suggest Citrix NetScaler. CVE-2025-7775 + CVE-2025-5777.
attackPaths.pivot: RCE on NetScaler; memory leak → session token extraction.
attackPaths.objective: Lateral move to production; fuel-procurement access.

attackPaths.name: Email spoofing → executive phishing
attackPaths.pivot: Phishing from cfo@ claiming urgent procurement; lure to qa.speed-hosted phishing.

paths.preAi: ADFS brute force standard; user enum via Kerberos error codes
paths.mythos: BPCL CMDs are state-espionage targets; ADFS is gateway to email + VPN; spraying breached creds from peer energy companies is high-ROI for foreign intelligence
paths.factor: ~3× weak password; 10× SMS MFA; near-invisible if leaked VPN creds used

paths.preAi: QA compromise textbook; default creds, data replication
paths.mythos: Test envs are forgotten frontier; adfstest likely manages prod-equivalent service accounts; QA DBs reverse-identifiable; production pivot with months undetected
paths.factor: ~5× isolated QA; 1× if shared service accounts; 10× if QA→Prod deployment pipeline

cisoPatchList.title: ADFS pre-auth hardening — disable form-based external login
cisoPatchList.fix: Disable form-based external login (Kerberos only). IP allowlist /adfs/ls/. Lockout 5 fails → 15 min. MFA TOTP/FIDO2.
cisoPatchList.validation: External form login blocked; lockout fires; MFA enforced

cisoPatchList.title: Citrix NetScaler patches CVE-2025-7775 + CVE-2025-5777
cisoPatchList.fix: Patch NetScaler to current build. TLS ≥ 1.2 enforced. Strong ciphers only.

cisoPatchList.title: QA / test environment segregation
cisoPatchList.fix: Dedicated QA service accounts (no prod reuse). Network isolation. Rotate all QA passwords. IP allowlist (developer IPs). Re-auth required for QA→Prod calls.
cisoPatchList.validation: AD audit confirms no shared service accounts; QA on isolated subnet

cisoPatchList.title: Email spoofing prevention — SPF -all + DMARC p=reject
cisoPatchList.fix: SPF ~all → -all. DMARC p=reject. DKIM. BIMI. Explicit SPF for mail subdomains.

cisoPatchList.title: Convenience portal SQLi remediation
cisoPatchList.fix: Parameterise queries. Cloudflare WAF SQLi rules. Data-integrity checks. API auth required.
cisoPatchList.validation: sqlmap finds 0 injection; WAF blocks SQLi PoC

cisoPatchList.title: Remote-access VPN — MFA + protocol audit
cisoPatchList.fix: MFA TOTP/FIDO2. Disable legacy protocols. Split-tunnel restrictions. 8-hour idle timeout. Geographic anomaly alerts.
cisoPatchList.validation: MFA enforced; protocol audit clean; geolocation alerts active

cisoPatchList.title: Subdomain enumeration audit on 30+ subdomains
cisoPatchList.fix: Inventory all subdomains. Verify each is authorised. Decommission unused. DNS integrity monitoring.
cisoPatchList.validation: Official subdomain register; monthly CT audit

phase2.headlineQuestion: With adfstest, qa.convenience, and qa.speed publicly enumerated and likely running weaker security, what controls prevent QA-environment compromise from pivoting to production — and are test credentials cleanly isolated from production databases?
